channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: JuJu Watkins and Paige Bueckers shine in matchup of two of women's basketball's top stars
item.description: HARTFORD, Conn. (AP) — JuJu Watkins and Paige Bueckers both showed out in a primetime matchup between two of the brightest stars in women's basketball. Watkins got the best of the rematch from last season's Elite Eight that UConn won, helping No.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FEINBERG, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Why Steve Kerr wants this play to have the Warriors talking
item.description: Even after a win over Minnesota, Steve Kerr had pointed remarks for his team about their style of play around Steph Curry.
item.dc:creator: Danny Emerman, The Mercury News

item.title: John Seidler takes over as control person of Padres after Peter's death
item.description: John Seidler, an entrepreneur and business executive, has been named the next control person of the San Diego Padres, pending approval by Major League Baseball, after the death of his brother Peter Seidler, the team's former owner.
item.dc:creator: Anna Ashcraft, Fox 5

item.title: Caitlin Clark effect hasn't reversed the decades-long decline in girls basketball participation
item.description: Nebraska's third-largest high school decided not to play varsity girls basketball this year after no juniors or seniors went out for the sport.
item.dc:creator: Josh Funk, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Day 3: Winter Classic Basketball
item.description: GRAND JUNCTION, Colo. (KREX)—Palisade High School was buzzing on Day 3 of the Winter Classic as the Fruita Wildcats faced off against the Palmer Ridge Bears. Fruita entered the game riding high after a recent win against Ponderosa, while Palmer Ridge looked to snap a three-game losing streak.
item.dc:creator: Titus Cleveland, KREX 5

item.title: Rickey Henderson: Oakland remembers the ballplayer who grew up in Bushrod
item.description: Henderson wasn't just one of the greatest to play baseball -- as he once acknowledged about himself. He was certifiably cool, radiating with the swagger Oaklanders see in themselves and their town.
item.dc:creator: Shomik Mukherjee, Jakob Rodgers, Rick Hurd, The Mercury News

item.title: Texas holds off Clemson's comeback to advance in College Football Playoff
item.description: The Texas Longhorns took care of business against the Clemson Tigers Saturday to advance to the quarterfinals of the College Football Playoff.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Texas holds off Clemson 38-24, sets up College Football Playoff quarterfinal against Arizona State
item.description: Jaydon Blue runs for 146 yards, scores two touchdowns to lead Texas past Clemson in College Football Playoff opener.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Lamar Jackson and the Ravens draw even atop the AFC North, beating the 1st-place Steelers 34-17
item.description: Lamar Jackson threw three touchdown passes and Marlon Humphrey scored on a fourth-quarter interception return to help the Baltimore Ravens pull even with Pittsburgh atop the AFC North with a 34-17 victory over the Steelers.
item.dc:creator: Noah Trister, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Mahomes throws for TD and runs for score as Chiefs beat Texans 27-19 to close in on No. 1 seed
item.description: KANSAS CITY, Mo. (AP) — Patrick Mahomes knew he could run on the ankle he sprained last week. The bigger concern for the Chiefs quarterback was how he would stop, when all the weight and pressure and momentum would land squarely upon it. The solution against Houston was simple: Mahomes didn't stop.
item.dc:creator: DAVE SKRETTA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: First baseman Paul Goldschmidt and the Yankees agree to a $12.5 million, 1-year deal, AP source says
item.description: First baseman Paul Goldschmidt and the New York Yankees agreed Saturday to a $12.5 million, one-year contract, a person familiar with the negotiations told The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: RONALD BLUM, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Former San Diego Padres player Rickey Henderson dies at 65: reports
item.description: Rickey Henderson, the man who changed Major League Baseball with his blazing, unbeatable speed, has died, according to multiple reports.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, Fox 5

item.title: Senate passes RFK Stadium land bill, giving the Washington Commanders a major off-the-field win
item.description: The path for the NFL's Washington Commanders to return to the nation’s capital is clear after an on-again, off-again saga in Congress ended early Saturday with a postmidnight reprieve. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN WHYNO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Philadelphia 76ers star Joel Embiid working through injuries and mental health struggles
item.description: PHILADELPHIA (AP) — Joel Embiid is trying to believe in the good that can come from asking for help. Embiid’s career has yielded an MVP — and so many more DNPs — with a biography littered by the kind of devastating injuries that can, in time, heal.
item.dc:creator: DAN GELSTON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: UCSD basketball heating up, Tritons on a tear after historic upset
item.description: Fresh after pulling off what might be one of the biggest wins in program history, UCSD men's basketball has no plans on slowing down.
item.dc:creator: Bri Savant, Fox 5

item.title: Winter Classic Day 3: Fruita bounces back, GJ Girls win thriller
item.description: Another loaded, action-packed day of hoops at the 2024 Family Health West Winter Classic is in the books.
item.dc:creator: Big Will Levenson, KREX 5

item.title: Caprock Hockey hangs tough with defending State Champion Durango
item.description: In their second-ever varsity home game, the Caprock Eagles once again had a tough test, this time with the defending 4A State Champion Durango Demons.
item.dc:creator: Big Will Levenson, KREX 5

item.title: The Thunder should have been quite tired. They went 2-0 in Florida anyway, pushing record to 22-5
item.description: MIAMI (AP) — Here's how the last few days have gone for the Oklahoma City Thunder: Lose the NBA Cup final to Milwaukee in Las Vegas on Tuesday, spend time airborne over four different time zones on Wednesday, win in Orlando on Thursday, win in Miami on Friday.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden admin withdraws proposed rule that would outlaw bans on trans athletes in girls' sports
item.description: Biden's Department of Education announced its withdrawal of a proposed rule that would have outlawed states from banning trans athletes in girls' sports.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Utes land talented running back Wayshawn Parker
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - The Utes football team may have picked up its biggest off-season acquisition yet. Washington State running back Wayshawn Parker, considered one of the top backs in the transfer portal, announced he will play for the Utes next season.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Cody Bellinger experienced World Series title with dad Clay at old Yankee Stadium
item.description: Bellinger won the 2019 NL MVP award with the Los Angeles Dodgers after hitting .305 with 47 homers, 115 RBIs and a 1.035 OPS.
item.dc:creator: RONALD BLUM, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: American competitive eater Joey Chestnut named Holiday Bowl Parade Grand Marshall
item.description: World-famous competitive eater Joey Chestnut has been chosen as the 2024 DIRECTV Holiday Bowl "Chief Eggnog Official." He will also serve as the Grand Marshal of the Holiday Bowl Parade.
item.dc:creator: Rhea Caoile, Fox 5

item.title: Lawmaker wants Chicago women's sports teams to have an even playing field
item.description: State Rep. Eva-Dina Delgado (D-Chicago) has introduced legislation requiring equity in public stadium funding for men’s and women’s sports. Illinois lawmakers began hearings this week on the issue.
item.dc:creator: Tahman Bradley, WGN 9

item.title: White Sox change name of ballpark to Rate Field
item.description: CHICAGO — The Chicago White Sox have changed the name of Guaranteed Rate Field to Rate Field. The name change is because the sponsor of the ballpark, Guaranteed Rate, changed their name to Rate.
item.dc:creator: Andy Koval, WGN 9

item.title: Tampa native named starting QB for Atlanta Falcons
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — The Atlanta Falcons are turning to rookie Michael Penix Jr. as their starting quarterback and benching veteran Kirk Cousins as they fight for their first playoff berth since 2017. Coach Raheem Morris announced the decision in a statement Tuesday night.
item.dc:creator: CHARLES ODUM AP Sports Writer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Guentzel scores in 7 consecutive games, 1 shy of matching the franchise record in Tuesday's win against Blue Jackets
item.description: TAMPA, Fla. (AP) — Nick Paul had a goal and two assists to lead the Tampa Bay Lightning to a 5-3 victory over the Columbus Blue Jackets. Jake Guentzel, Luke Glendening, Brayden Point and Mitchell Chaffee also scored for Tampa Bay, which has won five of six. Andrei Vasilevskiy finished with 28 saves.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Former Dodgers MVP heading to New York Yankees: report
item.description: The New York Yankees agreed to acquire outfielder Cody Bellinger from the Chicago Cubs on Tuesday for right-hander Cody Poteet, a person familiar with the negotiations told The Associated Press. The person spoke to the AP on condition of anonymity because the trade had not been announced.
item.dc:creator: RONALD BLUM, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: White Sox announce list of players to attend SoxFest 2025
item.description: The Chicago White Sox have announced a list of seven players and eight prospects who will join fans at SoxFest 2025, which is making its return after a four-year hiatus, featuring new fan experiences and a "State of the White Sox" address with Manager Will Venable and front office leadership.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Vucevic scores 24 points as Bulls hold off Raptors 122-121 for 4th win in 6 games
item.description: Nikola Vucevic scored 24 points, Coby White added 19 and the Chicago Bulls held on to beat the Toronto Raptors 122-121 on Monday night for their fourth win in six games.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: BYU excited to face Heisman winner Travis Hunter
item.description: PROVO, Utah (ABC4 Sports) - As BYU continues preparation for the Alamo Bowl December 28th against Colorado, they are looking forward to facing off against Heisman Trophy Winner Travis Hunter. Hunter is truly a one-of-a-kind player.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Josh Allen has another huge game to lift Bills to a 48-42 victory that snaps Lions' winning streak
item.description: Allen ran for two scores in the first quarter and threw two touchdown passes in the second half, leading the Buffalo Bills to a 48-42 victory Sunday that snapped the Detroit Lions ' franchise-record 11-game winning streak.
item.dc:creator: LARRY LAGE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Saquon Barkley slowed by 'minor' injury, loses ground in pursuit of Dickerson's record
item.description: Eagles running back Saquon Barkley had a setback in his chase of the NFL season rushing record when he appeared slowed by an undisclosed injury.
item.dc:creator: DAN GELSTON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Giants owner John Mara gets another aerial message trashing his team before loss to Ravens
item.description: New York Giants co-owner John Mara received another aerial message trashing his woeful team before Sunday's 35-14 loss to the Baltimore Ravens. It was the second straight week that a plane was hired to circle the stadium in protest of the Giants’ seventh losing season since 2017.
item.dc:creator: TOM CANAVAN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Miami WR DuBose taken off the field on a stretcher after hit to the head against Texans
item.description: Miami receiver Grant DuBose was hospitalized in stable condition and undergoing further evaluation after being taken off the field on a stretcher following a helmet-to-helmet hit against the Houston Texans on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: KRISTIE RIEKEN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Southampton fires Russell Martin after 5-0 rout by Spurs on a bad day for Premier League managers
item.description: Southampton fired manager Russell Martin late Sunday after the Premier League's last-placed team was thrashed 5-0 by Tottenham.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Freddie Freeman's walk-off grand slam ball sells for $1.56 million at auction
item.description: Freddie Freeman’s historic walk-off grand slam ball from Game 1 of the World Series has sold for $1.56 million at auction.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Shiffrin recovering after abdominal surgery to clean out deep puncture wound suffered in race crash
item.description: Mikaela Shiffrin is recovering after undergoing abdominal surgery on Thursday night to clean out a puncture wound she received in a giant slalom crash two weeks ago.
item.dc:creator: PAT GRAHAM, Associated Press, Fox 5
